vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF for #1595 PR 2 codeStructure schema/profile and validator

plan.title: feat(codebase): define codeStructure profile and validator

narratives.Description: PR 2 for #1595 defines the first concrete codeStructure metadata profile and validation behavior.
narratives.Origin: Derived from https://github.com/deftai/directive/issues/1595 after PR 1 (#1607) landed the source-of-truth decision record.
narratives.Overview:
## Summary

Implement the #1595 PR 2 slice: define a concrete codeStructure shape, validate it deterministically, and dogfood a minimal Directive-owned record.

## Plan Validity Check

The 2026-06-15 #1595 current-shape override resolved the physical home: canonical codeStructure lives only at PROJECT-DEFINITION.plan.architecture.codeStructure, typed through vbrief/schemas/vbrief-core.schema.json. Standalone paths are reserved for generated projections only, and x-directive/architecture.codeStructure remains a consumer fallback rather than Directive's dogfood home.

## Scope

- Add a codeStructure schema/profile for modules, path ownership, allowed patterns, projection manifest entries, optional file-purpose overrides, and optional glossary references.
- Add a validator for stable module IDs, safe relative globs/paths, module references, conflicting ownership declarations, projection manifest entries, and unknown-key tolerance.
- Add a minimal Directive dogfood record at PROJECT-DEFINITION.plan.architecture.codeStructure.
- Add task and tests for valid/invalid records.

## Non-Goals

- No deterministic extractor or provider handshake.
- No generated MAP projection.
- No local index or materialized-view implementation.
- No generated source headers.
- No consumer AGENTS/template propagation or hard task-check gate.

items.title: PR 2 scope is revalidated against the #1595 current-shape override and uses PROJECT-DEFINITION.plan.architecture.codeStructure as the canonical home.
codeStructure schema/profile defines the initial metadata shape.
Validator checks stable IDs, safe globs/paths, module references, ownership conflicts, projection manifest entries, and unknown-key tolerance.
Directive dogfoods a minimal PROJECT-DEFINITION codeStructure record.
Tests cover valid and invalid codeStructure records.
